2603.17205.title: OPERA: Online Data Pruning for Efficient Retrieval Model Adaptation
2603.17205.paper_summary: Domain-specific finetuning is essential for dense retrievers, yet not all training pairs contribute equally to the learning process. We introduce OPERA, a data pruning framework that exploits this heterogeneity to improve both the effectiveness and efficiency of retrieval model adaptation. We first investigate static pruning (SP), which retains only high-similarity query-document pairs, revealing an intrinsic quality-coverage tradeoff: ranking (NDCG) improves while retrieval (Recall) can degrade due to reduced query diversity. To resolve this tradeoff, we propose a two-stage dynamic pruning (DP) strategy that adaptively modulates sampling probabilities at both query and document levels throughout training, prioritizing high-quality examples while maintaining access to the full training set. Evaluations across eight datasets spanning six domains demonstrate the effectiveness of both approaches: SP improves ranking over standard finetuning (NDCG@10 +0.5\%), while DP achieves the strongest performance on both ranking (NDCG@10 +1.9\%) and retrieval (Recall@20 +0.7\%), with an average rank of 1.38 across all methods. These findings scale to Qwen3-Embedding, an LLM-based dense retriever, confirming architecture-agnostic benefits. Notably, DP reaches comparable performance in less than 50\% of the training time required by standard finetuning.
2603.17205.paper_summary_zh: 領域特定的微調對於密集檢索器至關重要，但並非所有訓練對都對學習過程貢獻相同。<lb/>我們介紹了 OPERA，一個數據修剪框架，利用這種異質性來提高檢索模型適應的有效性和效率。<lb/>我們首先研究靜態修剪（SP），該方法僅保留高相似度的查詢-文檔對，揭示了一種內在的質量-覆蓋權衡：排名（NDCG）改善，而檢索（召回率）可能因查詢多樣性的減少而下降。<lb/>為了解決這一權衡，我們提出了一種兩階段的動態修剪（DP）策略，在訓練過程中自適應地調整查詢和文檔層面的抽樣概率，優先考慮高質量的示例，同時保持對完整訓練集的訪問。<lb/>在跨越六個領域的八個數據集上的評估顯示了這兩種方法的有效性：SP 在標準微調上改善了排名（NDCG@10 +0.5\%），而 DP 在排名（NDCG@10 +1.9\%）和檢索（召回率@20 +0.7\%）上均達到了最佳性能，所有方法的平均排名為 1.38。<lb/>這些發現可擴展到 Qwen3-Embedding，一個基於 LLM 的密集檢索器，確認了與架構無關的好處。<lb/>值得注意的是，DP 在所需的標準微調訓練時間的不到 50\% 內達到了可比的性能。
2603.17205.author: Haoyang Fang et.al.
2603.17205.authors: Haoyang Fang, Shuai Zhang, Yifei Ma, Hengyi Wang, Cuixiong Hu, Katrin Kirchhoff, Bernie Wang, George Karypis
